title: Herbal tea for the lymphatic system
intro: Several herbal teas, including calendula, red clover, and echinacea, support the lymphatic system due to their anti-inflammatory, depurative, and immune-boosting properties. Cleansing and detoxifying the body, these teas help to eliminate waste and toxins, improving the lymphatic system's overall function. Moreover, burdock root and pokeroot teas have a long history of use as lymphatic system tonics. Regularly consuming these herbal teas strengthens the lymphatic system, contributing to better immune health and overall well-being.

conditions.tea_desc: Herbal teas, such as dandelion, burdock, and milk thistle, promote spleen health due to their respective properties and active constituents. Dandelion tea contains taraxacin, taraxacerin, and inulin, which activate the flow of bile. Burdock tea, with its arctiin and luteolin, possesses diuretic properties that remove toxins from the body. Milk thistle tea, enriched with silymarin, demonstrates antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. These herbal teas boost liver and gallbladder functions, thus alleviating stress on the spleen and reducing its enlargement.

Herbal teas assist in alleviating lymph node swelling due to their anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties. For instance, calendula tea, made from the petals of the marigold flower, contains flavonoids that possess anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling. Ginger tea, derived from the root of the ginger plant, has active constituents like gingerols and shogaols, which exhibit immunomodulatory effects, aiding in the reduction of lymph node swelling. Echinacea tea, extracted from the echinacea plant, contains alkylamides and cichoric acid, compounds known to stimulate the immune system and decrease inflammation, further helping with swollen lymph nodes. These herbal teas provide a natural and effective approach to managing lymph node swelling, thanks to their anti-inflammatory and immune-enhancing properties and active constituents.
